what_it_does: Proposes a reconciliation of the live color palette face against a 160-page source corpus, listing token and grammar augmentations, new token groups, computed rules, and open questions for the author.
observations: The file explicitly frames the work as a RECONCILE, not a mint, against an already-mature live palette face.
It maps source role names onto existing ramp positions: data-gold maps to accent.gold and title-gold maps to accent.ochre.
It surfaces one unresolved value conflict for figure-gold: live #E3C421 versus source-true ~#C8A800.
It adds a new color.semantic_rare group for transaction-green, boundary-maroon, directional greens/reds, and financial-outcome amber, kept separate from generic UI semantics.
It names the brand-white versus blank-white distinction and proposes a white-depth ladder inside the warm surface family.
It proposes a new warm-analogous chart_ramp derived by rotating hue from the accent toward orange-red, distinct from the ordinal axis_ramp.
It converts the existing gold-density prose dial into a computable per-register instance budget.
It ends with five open questions directed at Tim for decisions on values, registers, and token aliases.
standouts: The bright-vs-muted figure-gold value conflict is isolated and explicitly not auto-resolved.
The semantic_rare group is quarantined from generic UI semantic colors so scarcity meaning is not corrupted.
The blank-white (#FFFFFF) is treated like a foreign hue and restricted to overlay chrome and one glyph.
The chart_ramp is flagged as genuinely absent from the live face and derived from the accent hue rather than hand-placed.
conventions: Each augmentation uses a consistent schema: target, name, value_or_recipe, rationale, provenance, status, conflict_note.
Status labels are file-local and non-ranking: extends-existing, new, conflicts-with-existing.
Provenance cites specific collections, page numbers, and observed hex ranges.
Deployment rules that already belong in grammar are targeted there rather than duplicated into tokens.
The derive-never-place principle is repeated: title-gold is the next ordinal stop, chart colors rotate from the accent.
concerns: Many proposed hex values are given as observed ranges rather than a single locked canonical value.
Several new tokens depend on author decisions recorded only in the open_questions section.
chart_primary overlaps with the existing gold_soft token, creating a potential alias decision.
The file mixes token additions, grammar rule additions, computed recipes, and decision questions in one document.
notes: A dense, source-cited reconcile brief that mostly extends the existing palette while surfacing one unresolved gold-value conflict and several new token groups.
questions: What exactly does the live face contain for each targeted token, since only the proposed changes are shown?
Which artifact is the authoritative locked v2 mockup referenced in the gold-value conflict?
How are the register poles named and mapped to concrete artifact types beyond austere/investor and rich/marketing?
Where will Tim's answers to the open questions be recorded back into the ledger?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file is a reconcile proposal that maps source findings onto existing tokens and grammar and flags what still needs author decision.
apparent_themes: Warm-metal monochromatic palette with a single chromatic attention currency
Source-grounded provenance and computable density budgets
intention_signals: It tries to ground the existing palette in corpus evidence, make its rarity and density rules computable, and defer unresolved value choices to the author.
novelty: It treats color rarity as a lintable semantic budget and quarantines both foreign hues and pure white via explicit admission gates.
contribution: Source-grounded role-name aliases for the two gold tiers, a recorded unresolved value conflict for figure-gold, the semantic_rare group with its scarcity-as-meaning rule, the white-depth and blank-white quarantine, the warm-analogous chart_ramp derivation recipe, and a register-pole gold-instance budget.
summary_for_embedding: A proposed reconciliation document for a warm-metal design-system palette, mapping source-corpus findings onto existing color tokens and grammar, adding semantic_rare, white_depth, and chart_ramp groups, computed rarity budgets, and open questions for the author.
intention_for_embedding: To update the palette face by extending existing tokens and grammar with source-derived role names, new rare/chart/white token groups, and lintable rules while surfacing unresolved value conflicts for author decision.
